articles.title: Hollywood layoffs 2026: Disney, Sony, Bad Robot slash jobs in a bruising week for the entertainment industry
articles.summary: April 2026 has seen significant layoffs in Hollywood, with major studios and production companies like Bad Robot, Disney, and Sony cutting over 1,000 jobs combined. This comes amidst industry volatility driven by AI disruption, a changing business landscape, and consolidation. Bad Robot is also planning to downsize and relocate from Los Angeles to New York.

articles.title: Douglas Lane & Associates LLC Sells 8,416 Shares of The Walt Disney Company $DIS
articles.summary: Douglas Lane & Associates LLC decreased its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 1.5%, selling 8,416 shares and retaining 565,707 shares valued at $64.36 million. Disney reported stronger-than-expected earnings of $1.63 EPS and revenues of $25.98 billion in its last quarter, while analysts generally maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus price target of $132.19. The company faces streaming competition and margin pressures, leading management to implement cost-cutting measures, including job reductions.

articles.title: DIS Technical Analysis | Trend, Signals & Chart Patterns | WALT DISNEY CO/THE (NYSE:DIS)
articles.summary: This article provides a technical analysis of Walt Disney Co. (NYSE: DIS), assigning it an overall technical rating of 0 out of 10, indicating below-average market performance. While the long-term trend is negative and short-term is neutral, recent price consolidation and interest from large players suggest a potential setup opportunity. The analysis details support and resistance levels, various indicator signals, and identified candlestick and chart patterns.

articles.title: Disney's Paul Roeder Sets Team; April Carretta To Work For Dana Walden
articles.summary: Less than a month into his new role, Disney's Senior EVP and Chief Communications Officer, Paul Roeder, has announced the structure of his operation and leadership team. April Carretta has been promoted to EVP, Communications, and will add lead communications duties for Dana Walden, Disney Co.'s President and Chief Creative Officer, alongside her existing role for Direct-to-Consumer and tech & product teams. The new team includes David Jefferson leading corporate communications, Carrie Brown heading internal communications, and Mariana Vaca as Director, Communications, with key segment communication leads also reporting to Roeder.

The new team includes David Jefferson leading corporate communications, Carrie Brown heading internal communications, and Mariana Vaca as Director, Communications, with key segment communication leads also reporting to Roeder.

articles.title: 'Malcolm in the Middle: Life's Still Unfair' Cast Talks 'Magical' Reunion 25 Years in the Making
articles.summary: The cast of "Malcolm in the Middle" reunited for a four-episode revival series titled "Malcolm in the Middle: Life's Still Unfair," premiering on April 10, 2026, on Hulu and Disney+. Original cast members Bryan Cranston, Frankie Muniz, and Jane Kaczmarek discuss the surreal experience of returning to their roles after 25 years. The revival explores Malcolm's adult life, his family dynamics, and provides a sense of closure for the characters.

articles.title: Comcast (CMCSA) Stock: Strategic Streaming Bundle Expansion Strengthens Competitive Edge
articles.summary: Comcast (CMCSA) has enhanced its Xfinity StreamSaver bundle offerings by incorporating premium streaming platforms like Disney+, Hulu, and HBO Max, alongside existing services. This strategic move aims to boost customer loyalty and drive subscription growth by offering customizable packages with discounts up to 45%. The company's enhanced StreamStore platform and integrated service strategy strengthen its competitive edge in the evolving digital entertainment landscape.

articles.title: Exclusive | Disney Planning Layoffs Under New CEO Josh D’Amaro
articles.summary: Disney is reportedly planning significant layoffs under its new CEO, Josh D’Amaro, with up to 1,000 positions expected to be eliminated in the coming weeks. Many of these job cuts will occur within the company's recently consolidated marketing department. This move marks one of the first major actions by D'Amaro since taking on the role of chief executive.

articles.title: Report: Walt Disney Co. Plans Up to 1,000 Layoffs
articles.summary: Walt Disney Co. is reportedly planning to lay off up to 1,000 employees in the coming weeks as part of cost-cutting measures under new CEO Josh D’Amaro. These layoffs are primarily expected to impact Disney's recently consolidated marketing division. The company, like others in entertainment, is facing challenges with lower streaming profits, weaker box office returns, and increased competition.

articles.title: Xfinity says its new streaming bundles can cut bills by up to 45%
articles.summary: Comcast's Xfinity has significantly expanded its StreamSaver bundles, now including Disney+, Hulu, and HBO Max alongside Peacock, Netflix, and Apple TV. This allows customers to save up to 45% on streaming services, manage subscriptions on a single bill, and customize their entertainment packages through the updated StreamStore.
